title: Link-Bearing Elements, Base URLs, Relations, Discovery, and Scheduling · Behavior Case: Discovery Admission Order
summary: The crawler follows canonical, alternate, preload, form, script, mailto, and base-altered references as if every discovered URL were a navigational page. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the discovery admission order increment in typed link-discovery record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Resolve, validate syntax, remove fetch fragments, canonicalize conservatively, apply authorization and robots policy, then deduplicate before scheduling.
Interpret rel tokens such as canonical, alternate, next, prev, nofollow, sponsored, and ugc as evidence or hints, not universal commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-discovery-admission-order — Resolve, validate syntax, remove fetch fragments, canonicalize conservatively, apply authorization and robots policy, then deduplicate before scheduling.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-link-discovery-relations-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-link-discovery-relations-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_link_discovery_relations_5(raw_references: int = 36, resolved_references: int = 34, scheduled_pages: int = 18, rejected_references: int = 16, unexplained_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if raw_references < 1 or resolved_references < 0:
        return False, resolved_references, "link-count"
    if resolved_references > raw_references:
        return False, resolved_references, "resolution-accounting"
    if scheduled_pages + rejected_references != resolved_references:
        return False, scheduled_pages, "admission-reconciliation"
    if unexplained_decisions > 0:
        return False, unexplained_decisions, "link-provenance"
    assert scheduled_pages + rejected_references == resolved_references
    return True, scheduled_pages, "typed-link-discovery"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Resolve, validate syntax, remove fetch fragments, canonicalize conservatively, apply authorization and robots policy, then deduplicate before scheduling.
